name: Bezal Local Commerce MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server providing search access to 7,000+ local service businesses across America. AI agents can search by category, location, or service type to find local businesses — enabling location-aware commerce recommendations, service discovery, and local business intelligence workflows.
use_cases: Finding local service providers by category and location for consumer recommendation agents
Building local business discovery features in AI assistants and chatbots
Researching local service market density for business intelligence agents
Powering hyper-local commerce recommendations from agent-based applications
not_for: Real-time business availability or booking (directory data only)
Large enterprise or national chain businesses (local focus)
best_when: A consumer AI assistant or local services platform wants to help users find local service businesses — contractors, cleaners, plumbers, restaurants — using a curated US business directory.
avoid_when: You need global business data or real-time availability. The 7,000 business dataset is specialized for US local services specifically.

auth.notes: Remote MCP endpoint. Authentication details available from Bezal. API key likely required.

pricing.notes: Commercial service — pricing not publicly documented. 7,000 curated business listings is a proprietary dataset.

known_agent_gotchas: Dataset limited to 7,000 US businesses — coverage may be sparse in some areas
Business data freshness unknown — listings may include closed businesses
Remote SaaS endpoint — availability depends on Bezal service uptime
Limited documentation makes it hard to evaluate data quality and coverage
Niche commercial service — validate data accuracy before production use
agent_readiness.security_notes: Business directory data — generally low sensitivity. User location queries may reveal personal location preferences. API key protection required.

security_score_components.security_notes: Business directory — low sensitivity data. Location queries may infer user location patterns. Remote endpoint HTTPS.
